localStorage for JWT token and basket ID storage

DOM manipulation (template binding, innerHTML equivalent patterns)

controls: express-jwt@0.1.3 isAuthorized() middleware — applied on subset of routes
express-rate-limit — only on /rest/user/reset-password and 3 other endpoints

Support user credential at routes/login.ts:61 — **** (31 chars)
known_vulns: SQL injection at routes/login.ts:34 — raw string interpolation in SELECT
SQL injection at routes/search.ts:23 — LIKE clause with criteria from req.query.q
eval(code) SSTI at routes/userProfile.ts:62 — executes arbitrary JS in Node.js context
SSRF at routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts:24 — fetch(req.body.imageUrl) no allowlist
21 endpoints missing authentication (missing_auth_suspect=true)
18 endpoints missing authorization (IDOR patterns)
express-jwt@0.1.3 — no algorithms allowlist, alg:none bypass enabled
jsonwebtoken@0.4.0 — critically outdated, CVE-2022-23529
MD5 password hashing at lib/insecurity.ts:43 — no salt
1024-bit RSA key (below NIST 2048-bit minimum)
In-memory session map — no server-side logout
Admin configuration endpoints unauthenticated

POST /profile/image/file — profile image file upload (missing auth)

GET /ftp/:file and GET /ftp/ — file server with directory listing
GET /logs/:file — log file server (user-controlled filename)
GET /ftp/quarantine/:file — quarantine file server
controls: keyServer: checks for '/' in filename to prevent path traversal (partial)
File extension allowlist for profile images (jpg/jpeg/png/svg/gif)
known_vulns: XXE at routes/fileUpload.ts:83 — libxmljs2 parseXml with noent:true (external entity resolution)
SSRF at routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts:24 — fetch(url) with no URL allowlist
Path traversal at routes/logfileServer.ts:14 — path.resolve('logs/', file) with user-controlled file
Path traversal at routes/quarantineServer.ts:14 — path.resolve('ftp/quarantine/', file)
Public key exposure at routes/keyServer.ts — serves jwt.pub (RSA public key) and premium.key without auth
Directory listing at /ftp/ via serve-index — exposes file listing
File type validation by extension only — MIME type mismatch attacks possible
POST /file-upload missing authentication — anyone can upload

notevil library as 'safe eval' (CVE-prone, sandbox escape known)
RSA RS256 JWT verification (but private key is hardcoded and public)

known_vulns: RCE via vm.runInContext sandbox escape at routes/b2bOrder.ts:23 — notevil is not RCE-safe
JWT bearer required but private key committed to source — authentication bypassed trivially

YAML injection possible if orderLinesData contains YAML-like structures
No rate limiting on B2B order endpoint
Sandbox timeout only 2000ms — DoS via slow operations within notevil budget

interfaces: Sequelize 6.37.3 ORM interface — models/user.ts, models/product.ts, etc.
Raw sequelize.query() calls bypassing ORM in security-critical paths

SQLite file-based storage (juice-shop.db or in-memory for tests)
Filesystem read/write for uploads/, ftp/, encryptionkeys/, logs/
serve-index directory listing for /ftp/
jwt.pub RSA public key served at /encryptionkeys/jwt.pub
controls: SQLite database — single file, no network, no auth
chgrp/chmod applied to ftp/, frontend/dist/, logs/, data/, i18n/ in Dockerfile
known_secrets: RSA private key NOT stored in database but jwt.pub is served publicly
SQLite Users table contains all user hashed passwords (MD5, no salt)
known_vulns: SQL injection sink at raw sequelize.query() in routes/login.ts and routes/search.ts
NoSQL injection potential in MarsDB queries if user input passes unvalidated
No database-level access control — single SQLite file with full read/write by app process
User passwords stored as unsalted MD5 hashes — rainbow table attack trivial
Encryption keys directory served publicly — jwt.pub enables offline JWT analysis
No at-rest encryption for SQLite database containing user PII and credentials
GDPR/data minimization concerns — data-export endpoint missing auth
